$schema_note: states: not_started -> active -> blocked -> passing; only a successful run of `verification` may set state to passing

features.behavior: profiler/timing.cu provides reusable GpuTimer (cudaEvent start/stop, elapsed ms); kernels/00_elementwise_add.cu compiles standalone, runs correctly, and reports elapsed ms via GpuTimer
features.verification: python scripts/check.py && nvcc -arch=sm_89 kernels/00_elementwise_add.cu -o build/00_elementwise_add.exe && build\00_elementwise_add.exe

features.behavior: profiler/nvml_specs.cpp prints peak FP32 TFLOP/s and memory bandwidth for device 0 via NVML, falling back to CLAUDE.md desktop-4070 numbers on failure; output is JSON
features.verification: python scripts/check.py && build\nvml_specs.exe outputs valid JSON with tflops>0 and bandwidth_gbs>0

features.behavior: kernels/01_naive_gemm.cu and kernels/02_tiled_gemm.cu compile standalone, verify results against CPU reference, and report analytic FLOPs (2*M*N*K) plus elapsed ms
features.verification: python scripts/check.py && both gemm exes run with exit code 0 and print PASS on correctness check

features.behavior: profiler/cupti_counters.cpp exposes measure_dram_bytes(kernel_launch_fn) returning dram__bytes.sum per launch; kernels remain CUPTI-unaware
features.verification: python scripts/check.py && cupti test exe reports nonzero dram bytes for a known-size copy kernel within 2x of analytic bytes

features.behavior: kernels/03_softmax.cu and kernels/04_naive_attention.cu compile standalone, pass CPU-reference correctness checks, report analytic FLOPs and bytes
features.verification: python scripts/check.py && both exes run with exit code 0 and print PASS

features.behavior: cli/roofline.py runs each kernel exe, collects (kernel_name, arithmetic_intensity, achieved_flops), writes results/<kernel>.json
features.verification: python scripts/check.py && python cli/roofline.py --all exits 0 and results/*.json exist with required keys

features.behavior: plot/plot_roofline.py renders log-log roofline.png from results JSON: compute roof, bandwidth roof, ridge point label, one scatter point per kernel
features.verification: python scripts/check.py && python cli/roofline.py --all produces roofline.png containing all kernel points

features.behavior: kernels/05_flash_attention.cu (FA2 tiled, online softmax, causal mask) passes correctness vs naive attention and appears on the same roofline plot visibly closer to the compute roof
features.verification: python scripts/check.py && FA exe prints PASS vs naive reference && roofline.png shows both attention points with FA at higher arithmetic intensity
